commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Manual maintainer review is needed because this protected-label PR changes Telegram authorization/upgrade behavior and needs proof acceptance, not a narrow mechanical repair.; Cleared: No concrete supply-chain, secrets, or new code-execution concern was found; the security-sensitive behavior change is covered as merge risk and proof gating. (sha=44a13f7e41056e8681b6aa3d4f94655981e0f156)
commands.review_summary:
The PR requires Telegram target writeback to have `operator.admin` unless the call is a Telegram-private internal send path with no gateway scopes.

PR surface: Source +73, Tests +28. Total +101 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, but I did not execute a live repro. Current main lets undefined Telegram gateway scopes fall through the writeback guard, and durable inbound delivery can forward missing `GatewayClientScopes` into that path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual maintainer review is needed because this protected-label PR changes Telegram authorization/upgrade behavior and needs proof acceptance, not a narrow mechanical repair.

target.title: fix(telegram): require admin for target writeback [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

- Requires `operator.admin` gateway scope before Telegram target resolution writes back to user config or cron delivery targets.
- Treats missing durable inbound gateway scopes as an empty scope set, so channel-originated missing scope facts fail closed for writeback.
- Preserves legacy direct/internal Telegram target normalization through a Telegram-owned private `trustedInternalWriteback` path, without adding a shared/plugin-facing internal authority field.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes none.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related maintainer-requested hardening for Telegram target writeback authorization.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Yes.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram target writeback no longer treats missing gateway scope facts as authorization to persist config or cron target normalization. Non-admin and empty gateway scopes deny writeback; `operator.admin` allows writeback; direct/internal Telegram sends preserve legacy target normalization through a Telegram-private trusted path.
- Real environment tested: Local source checkout using focused Vitest coverage for Telegram target writeback, Telegram send resolution, and durable inbound reply delivery. No live Telegram bot/API run was performed.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/targets.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts src/channels/turn/durable-delivery.test.ts`; `git diff --check`; `rg "targetWritebackAuthority" . --glob '!node_modules/**' --glob '!dist/**'`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --prompt "Review the Telegram target writeback hardening for PR 88973. Prioritize regressions and security boundary issues. The intended resolution is narrow: missing gateway scopes deny config/cron writeback, explicit operator.admin allows writeback, trusted internal Telegram writeback is private to Telegram send resolution, and no shared/plugin-facing internal authority flag remains."`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Focused Vitest passed: `Test Files 2 passed (2), Tests 151 passed (151)` for the Telegram shard; `Test Files 1 passed (1), Tests 6 passed (6)` for the channels shard; wrapper result: `[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 32.59s`. `git diff --check` passed. `rg "targetWritebackAuthority" ...` returned no matches. Autoreview reported `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported` and `overall: patch is correct (0.84)` on head `44a13f7e41056e8681b6aa3d4f94655981e0f156`.
- Observed result after fix: Missing, empty, and non-admin gateway scopes skip Telegram config/cron writeback; `operator.admin` still writes back; direct/internal Telegram send resolution passes the private `trustedInternalWriteback` marker; durable inbound replies pass missing gateway scopes as `[]`.
- What was not tested: Live Telegram API behavior, live gateway-to-Telegram flow, and redacted config/cron mutation logs from a real bot setup were not exercised.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Proof is focused local source behavior with mocked Telegram/config/cron boundaries plus structured autoreview. This does not satisfy the live Telegram proof standard by itself.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Current main only skips writeback when `gatewayClientScopes` is an array missing `operator.admin`; `undefined` falls through and can persist target normalization.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/targets.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts src/channels/turn/durable-delivery.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `rg "targetWritebackAuthority" . --glob '!node_modules/**' --glob '!dist/**'`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --prompt "Review the Telegram target writeback hardening for PR 88973. Prioritize regressions and security boundary issues. The intended resolution is narrow: missing gateway scopes deny config/cron writeback, explicit operator.admin allows writeback, trusted internal Telegram writeback is private to Telegram send resolution, and no shared/plugin-facing internal authority flag remains."`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added target-writeback coverage for missing, empty, non-admin, admin, and non-admin-plus-internal-marker cases. Added direct Telegram send coverage proving internal send resolution still marks private trusted writeback. Added durable inbound reply coverage proving missing gateway scopes are forwarded as `[]`.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The previous writeback guard allowed missing scope facts to authorize target persistence. An earlier version of this PR also introduced a shared/plugin-facing `targetWritebackAuthority` propagation path; the current head removes that shared authority surface and keeps trusted internal writeback local to Telegram send resolution.

If no test was added, why not?

Not applicable.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No schema or migration change. Runtime writeback authorization changes for Telegram target normalization in user config and cron delivery targets.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

What is the highest-risk area?

Preventing missing or non-admin gateway scope facts from mutating saved Telegram config/cron targets while preserving intended direct/internal Telegram target normalization.

How is that risk mitigated?

The writeback sink requires `operator.admin` or a Telegram-private trusted internal marker. Gateway scope facts dominate: if a scope array is present and lacks `operator.admin`, writeback is denied even if the private internal marker is present. Durable inbound missing scopes normalize to `[]`, and no shared/plugin-facing `targetWritebackAuthority` field remains.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review should decide whether mocked/local proof is acceptable for this hardening PR or whether to require live Telegram/equivalent runtime proof before merge.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Current head is `44a13f7e41056e8681b6aa3d4f94655981e0f156`. Local focused tests and autoreview passed. Live Telegram proof was not run. GitHub check rollup should still be reviewed before merge.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Addressed the ClawSweeper findings about keeping internal writeback authority out of plugin/shared channel contexts and making explicit gateway scope facts dominate the writeback decision.
target.author: pgondhi987
